# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.135.0/http/server.ts";
const port = 8080; // set the server to listen on this port

async function handler(req: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const url = new URL(req.url);
  
  let modulePath = url.searchParams.get('module');
  
  if (!modulePath) {
    modulePath = Deno.env.get("MODULE_PATH"); // get module path from environment variables
  }

  try {
    const mod = await import(`file://${Deno.cwd()}/${modulePath}`); // dynamically load the module
    
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ success: true, message: "Module loaded successfully" }, null, 2));
  } catch (error) {
    console.error(error);
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ success: false, error: `Error loading module: ${modulePath}` }, null, 2), { status: 500 });
  }
}

console.log(`Listening on http://localhost:${port}/ Use the parameter '?module=yourModuleName' or set environment variable MODULE_PATH to load a module`);
await serve(handler, { port }); // start the server with the handler function
